pi_items.source: Confirmed in setup_openlca.sh:28, setup_task.sh:29. Original data downloaded from https://github.com/FLCAC-admin/uslci-content/raw/dev/downloads/uslci_fy24_q4_01_olca2_4_0_elci_lib_json_ld.zip (NREL/FLCAC repository)
Confirmed in export_result.sh:12 and task description. Directory /home/ga/LCA_Results created in setup_task.sh:22

Task description states 'reduce the Carbon dioxide output emission to approximately 10% of its original value (representing 90% capture)'. 90% CO2 capture is a real-world standard for industrial CCS retrofits (e.g., NRG's WA Parish project). Verifier checks for ratio 0.05-0.20 (5-20% of original), centered on target of ~10% (90% reduction).

Value not directly used in verifier.py or setup scripts. Possible interpretation: tolerance represents 15 percentage points range width, matching verifier's hardcoded ratio range 0.05-0.20 (20%-5% = 15 percentage points). However, no explicit validation or usage found.

pi_items.verified_value: USLCI FY24 Q4 from NREL/FLCAC (Federal LCA Commons)
pi_items.source: install_openlca.sh:175 downloads from https://github.com/FLCAC-admin/uslci-content repository. USLCI is the U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database maintained by NREL, a collaboration between DOE, EPA, and USDA.
USLCI database is a real, peer-reviewed LCA dataset from NREL containing actual US production and supply chain data. Not synthetic. Confirmed through NREL/FLCAC documentation and GitHub repository.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory) database FY24 Q4 from NREL/FLCAC, downloaded from GitHub. The task correctly specifies a 90% CO2 reduction target (reducing CO2 to ~10% of original), which matches real-world CCS (Carbon Capture & Storage) retrofit standards. File paths are verified: USLCI zip at /home/ga/LCA_Imports/uslci_database.zip, report output at /home/ga/LCA_Results/ccs_retrofit_report.txt. The verifier enforces CO2 ratio between 0.05-0.20 (5-20% of original), accepting reductions that target approximately 90% capture efficiency, a standard for industrial CCS applications.
